* fix: forecast not found after job finishes due to JSONB key-order hash mismatch

refresh_data_source() was calling get_or_create_source() with attributes
loaded from PostgreSQL JSONB. JSONB serializes JSON object keys in alphabetical
order, but attributes_hash was originally computed from the Python insertion-
order dict — so the hash never matched and a brand-new DataSource was created
with a different ID. The forecasts were saved under the new ID while the job
meta still recorded the original ID, causing the search to return empty.

Fix: look up the data source by its ID first (via db.session.get), falling back
to get_or_create_source only if the ID lookup fails.

Also fix dep.decode("utf-8") in get_forecast: modern RQ returns dependency_ids
as plain strings, not bytes, so guard with isinstance(dep, bytes) before
decoding.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/FlexMeasures/flexmeasures/sessions/7c09fac4-ff01-4f61-9631-7b5218d026a9

Co-authored-by: nhoening <1042336+nhoening@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove inline db import (already at top), simplify dep decode expression

Co-authored-by: nhoening <1042336+nhoening@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make DataSource.hash_attributes stable under JSON key order (sort_keys=True)

PostgreSQL JSONB always returns object keys in alphabetical order, so a dict
created with Python's insertion order (e.g. {"z": 1, "a": 2}) comes back from
the DB as {"a": 2, "z": 1}. The old hash_attributes used json.dumps without
sort_keys, meaning the two orderings produced different SHA-256 digests.
get_or_create_source therefore failed to find an existing DataSource and
silently inserted a duplicate row with a different ID.

Changes:
- DataSource.hash_attributes: add sort_keys=True to json.dumps
- DataSource.__init__: reuse hash_attributes() (DRY)
- Migration a5b26c3f8e91: recompute all existing attributes_hash values
  using the new sort_keys=True function
- test_data_source: add test_get_or_create_source_stable_under_key_order
  to prove the bug and guard against regression

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/FlexMeasures/flexmeasures/sessions/76c6b0aa-22f8-4bbc-8b54-d2a689546d68

Co-authored-by: Flix6x <30658763+Flix6x@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(migration): handle duplicate DataSource rows and simplify downgrade

Upgrade: before updating attributes_hash values, detect groups of DataSource
rows that share the same normalised unique key (after JSONB sorting). For each
such conflict group the newest row (highest id) is kept clean; older duplicates
get a synthetic {"flexmeasures-hash-conflict": N} attribute so their hash stays
unique -- avoiding a UniqueConstraint violation without touching timed_belief.

Downgrade: removed the hash-recomputation loop. PostgreSQL JSONB stores object
keys in alphabetical order, so json.dumps(attrs) and
json.dumps(attrs, sort_keys=True) produce the same string for any JSONB-round-
tripped attributes -- making the old loop a no-op. The downgrade is now an
intentional pass with an explanatory docstring.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/FlexMeasures/flexmeasures/sessions/c6f63d53-58a6-4dc3-9710-7e5846770626

Co-authored-by: Flix6x <30658763+Flix6x@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: bypass stats cache when no data found, so freshly-uploaded data is visible immediately

get_sensor_stats used a 120-second TTL LRU cache.  When a sensor had no data on
first load, the empty result was cached.  Uploading data within the 120-second
window then had no visible effect: the forecast dialog kept showing "no data"
and could not auto-populate the start datetime.

Fix: after getting an empty result from the cache, immediately do a second call
with a unique ttl_hash (time.time(), which is never reused) to force a fresh
database query.  This bypasses the stale empty cache entry without affecting
the 120-second caching for sensors that already have data.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/FlexMeasures/flexmeasures/sessions/ccc82d3a-6e28-445e-bfa9-3bff5c0483ef

Co-authored-by: nhoening <1042336+nhoening@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: re-populate normal cache slot after empty-sensor bypass to prevent repeated DB hits

After a successful bypass (empty cached result but fresh DB query finds data),
clear the stale empty LRU entry and immediately re-populate the normal TTL
slot. Subsequent calls within the 120-second window are now served from cache
instead of hitting the database each time.

The cache_clear() is acceptable here because the event is infrequent (a sensor
transitioning from empty to non-empty) and all other sensors' non-empty cache
entries will simply be re-populated on their next request.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/FlexMeasures/flexmeasures/sessions/aeb32b46-e0b2-4a83-922b-e8a299536c22

Co-authored-by: nhoening <1042336+nhoening@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace global lru_cache with per-key dict TTL cache for sensor stats

The previous fix used cache_clear() on a global lru_cache to evict stale
empty entries, which itself opened a DoS window: any caller could trigger
repeated global evictions.

Replace with a module-level dict (_sensor_stats_cache) that maps
(sensor_id, ...) keys to (timestamp, result) pairs:
- Non-empty results are cached per key for 120 seconds (same TTL as before)
- Empty results are stored in the dict but immediately treated as expired on
  the next call, so data uploaded to a previously-empty sensor is always
  visible without any global eviction
- Only the specific per-key entry is ever overwritten; no other sensor's
  cached data is affected

Removes: @lru_cache(), ttl_hash parameter, _get_ttl_hash() helper, and
the cache_clear() call introduced in b6576a3f / a71a975d.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/FlexMeasures/flexmeasures/sessions/a8859a89-bf08-40ee-9520-4a359ab3d0de

Co-authored-by: nhoening <1042336+nhoening@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add max-size (1000) and time-bucket key to sensor stats cache

- Include round(time.time() / 120) in the cache key so at most one DB hit
  per sensor per 120-second window is possible; this also caps the number
  of unique keys an attacker can create to 1 per sensor per bucket, limiting
  cache-exhaustion risk.
- Cap the dict at _SENSOR_STATS_MAX_SIZE=1000 entries; when full, the oldest
  entry (FIFO insertion order) is evicted before inserting a new one.
- Empty results are still never cached (freshly-uploaded data always visible).
- Removes the explicit (populated_at, result) tuple -- TTL is now implicit in
  the bucket component of the key, so only the result dict is stored.

chore(deps): update patch/minor dependencies (playwright 1.58.2, node 24.14.1) (#3391)

Renovate patch/minor dependency update with puppeteer excluded and a
yarn.lock fix for playwright type conflicts.

## Changes

- **Excluded**: `puppeteer` bump (`24.36.1` → `24.40.0`) — kept at
`24.36.1`
- **Updated**: `playwright` / `@playwright/browser-*` `1.58.1` →
`1.58.2`, `node` `24.13.0` → `24.14.1`,
`@apify/docusaurus-plugin-typedoc-api` `5.1.0` → `5.1.3`
- **Fixed**: yarn.lock had stale entries resolving `playwright@^1.52.0`
→ `1.58.1` and `playwright-core@^1.54.1` → `1.58.1` (used by
`@browserbasehq/stagehand`'s transitive deps) while the root used
`1.58.2`. TypeScript treated the two `playwright-core` copies as
structurally distinct, causing type errors in stagehand tests.
Consolidated all specifiers to `1.58.2`.

commit.message:
perf(maglev): eliminate dead overflow checks for i32-range arithmetic

i32 + i32 always fits in i64, so the 64-bit JO (overflow) check can
never fire.  Remove the JO + JMP pair and the entire slow path for
GenericAdd/Sub/Mul/Inc/Dec/Negate/BitwiseOr/And/Xor/Not when both
operands are provably i32.

This reduces the i32 fast path from 7 instructions to 4 per add
(load left, load right, op, store) — a 43% reduction in hot loop
instruction count.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
EXP-1231-1240: pipeline diagnostics and clinical validation

Results:
- EXP-1231: Ensemble alignment bug (documented, EXP-1211 SOTA stands)
- EXP-1232: ACF(1)=0.48, ACF(6)≈0 — AR only works at short horizons
- EXP-1233: Dawn conditioning provides zero benefit (-0.001)
- EXP-1234: Conformal PIs perfectly calibrated (80%→79.7%, 90%→89.9%, 95%→95.3%)
- EXP-1235: U-shaped error profile (Q3 lowest, Q4 highest)
- EXP-1236: 3-model ensemble optimal (+0.028), 5-model adds nothing
- EXP-1237: AR coefficients ≈ 0 at 60-min horizon (confirms ACF analysis)
- EXP-1238: Online learning marginal (+0.004, helps high-NaN patients)
- EXP-1239: Systematic +5mg bias at low glucose (calibration opportunity)
- EXP-1240: MARD=20.1%, Clarke Zone A=64.1%, False Safe=0.63%

Key insight: Ensemble+AR works because short-horizon sub-models
have recent AR residuals where autocorrelation is still significant.
At 60-min lag, autocorrelation vanishes completely.

commit.message:
[iOS] Fix ObjectDisposedException in ContentView.RemoveContentMask (#34680)

## Description

Fixes an `ObjectDisposedException` crash in
`ContentView.RemoveContentMask()` on iOS/MacCatalyst when the underlying
`CAShapeLayer` (`_contentMask`) has already been deallocated by the
native runtime during view hierarchy teardown.

### The Problem

During view lifecycle teardown, `WillRemoveSubview()` calls
`RemoveContentMask()`, which calls `RemoveFromSuperLayer()` on
`_contentMask`. When iOS has already collected the native
`CAShapeLayer`, the managed wrapper's `Handle` is `IntPtr.Zero`, calling
any method on it throws `ObjectDisposedException`.

This is a manifestation of the long-standing iOS binding lifecycle issue
documented in
[dotnet/macios#10562](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/issues/10562),
where `dealloc` of a UIView calls `RemoveFromSuperview()` for every
child while the native object is being deallocated, causing managed code
to interact with already-disposed native objects.

**Crash stack:**
```
ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a disposed object.
Object name: 'Microsoft.Maui.Platform.StaticCAShapeLayer'.
   at ObjCRuntime.NativeObjectExtensions.GetNonNullHandle(INativeObject, String)
   at CoreAnimation.CALayer.RemoveFromSuperLayer()
   at Microsoft.Maui.Platform.ContentView.RemoveContentMask()
   at Microsoft.Maui.Platform.ContentView.WillRemoveSubview(UIView)
```

### The Fix

Added a disposal guard in `RemoveContentMask()` checking
`_contentMask.Handle != IntPtr.Zero` before calling
`RemoveFromSuperLayer()`. This follows the established pattern used
throughout the codebase in `ViewRenderer.cs`, `LayoutFactory2.cs`,
`CellRenderer.cs`, `SkiaGraphicsView.cs`, etc.

### Related Work

- dotnet/macios#10562 (upstream iOS binding lifecycle issue)
- #30861, #33818 (prior iOS disposal safety fixes in MAUI)

### Testing

Added device test `RemoveContentMaskDoesNotThrowWhenDisposed` that:
1. Creates a `ContentView` with an active clip mask
2. Asserts the mask was created as a `CAShapeLayer`
3. Disposes the native handle (simulating iOS deallocation) and asserts
`Handle == IntPtr.Zero`
4. Verifies `RemoveFromSuperview()` does not throw

---------

commit.message:
fix(maglev): add missing JMP to skip slow path in i32-range arithmetic

The i32-range optimization (d94caf2d) had a fall-through bug: after
JO slow_label, the fast path fell through directly into the slow path
code instead of jumping to done_label. This meant every i32-range
arithmetic operation was unconditionally calling the full runtime stub,
negating the optimization entirely.

Add JMP done_label after each JO/JCC check in the i32 fast path for
GenericAdd/Sub/Mul/Inc/Dec/Negate.
